text:
The Goddess Advent Cards (「女神降臨」のカード, "Megami Kōrin" no Kādo) are a collection of 5 Nen-imbued cards that the Card Hunter Ogyu seeks to acquire. Hunter × Hunter: Altar of Dragon Vein 

Overview

The Card Hunter Ogyu seeks to acquire all five of these cards in order for him to be able to grant his one true wish. 

Cards

The cameo of Goddess

The cameo of Goddess (女神のカメオ, Megami no Kameo) is the first of the five cards shown in the game. It is obtained after fighting a group of Kiriko's in the Wollow Forest.

Item Description: The cameo of Goddess. It's the very rare card!

The ring of Goddess

The ring of Goddess (女神のリング, Megami no Ringu) is the second of the five cards shown in the game. It is obtained after fighting an unnamed mage and its skeleton puppets in the Underground Ruins.

Item Description: The ring of Goddess. It's the very rare card!

The tiara of Goddess

The tiara of Goddess (女神のティアラ, Megami no Tiara) is the third of the five cards shown in the game. It is obtained after fighting a group of unnamed humanoid frogs in the Crystal Cave.

Item Description: The tiara of Goddess. It's the very rare card!

The bracelet of Goddess

The bracelet of Goddess (女神のブレスレット, Megami no Buresuretto) is the fourth of the five cards shown in the game. It is obtained after fighting a group of unnamed giant humanoids in the Forest Ruins.

Item Description: The bracelet of Goddess. It's the very rare card!

The earrings of Goddess

The earrings of Goddess (女神のイヤリング, Megami no Iyaringu) is the fifth and last of the five cards shown in the game. It is obtained after fighting Amane and an unconscious Zegin Highline under Amane's control in the Zegin's Mansion.

Item Description: The earrings of Goddess. It's the very rare card!

Plot

Altar of Dragon Vein

 Note: Events occurring in Altar of Dragon Vein do not constitute canon material. Furthermore, some events may slightly differ depending on the current player character. 

After Ogyu manages to collect four out of five cards, Zegin Highline and his group steals them from him. He soon comes across Gon and Killua. Mistaking them for Zegin's henchmen, he attacks them. After the battle, the boys explain themselves and bring Ogyu to the airship. There, Ogyu explains how the Goddess Advent Cards function and his intent on preventing them from falling into the wrong hands. He then joins the group and seeks out said cards. 

When they manage to collect all of the cards, they realize that one of them was the one Ogyu did not manage to collect. They seek out the final card in Zegin's Mansion. This leads to a confrontation with the once-thought deceased Amane, who fulfills his goal of using Zegin's unconscious body for his own use. Amane and the manipulated corpse of Zegin fight the Hunter group and die in the aftermath. 

After obtaining all five Goddess Advent Cards, Ogyu summons the Goddess from the cards. He then wishes to travel the world and find the cards again, resulting in them scattering. When questioned, he explains that he prefers to find the cards alone. With that done, Ogyu goes off on another adventure but not before giving a ride to Romaria to the Midonite Government Environment Protection Bureau. 

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

The cards function similarly to the seven Dragon Balls from the manga series Dragon Ball. Both are a number of items that grant any wish to the person who gathered them.
